description: During their sparring session, May pushes Elena to confront her most painful memories and guilt, specifically a childhood trauma involving her cousin and uncle. This revelation brings Elena one step closer to breaking through her psychological barriers. Meanwhile, Afterlife is thrown into chaos as Kora's cell is found scorched and she goes missing.

transcript.line: 10, 11. My dad got mixed up with some bad people, and, uh... I went to stay with my uncle and my cousin Francisco to get away from it. But one of them tracked us down.
I should never have taken it. I should've stayed still.
You were a child. Okay, um, you're impulsive, but that's not always a bad thing. I think the point of this ridiculous exercise is to forgive yourself for it.
Our visitor, did you find out how her gift was suppressed?
It doesn't seem to be any kind of substance.
Then it was all for nothing?
It's too late. There's only ever been one solution to this problem.
What did you do to her?
We didn't do anything. Kora did this herself.
Ever since she went through the mist, she's been unable to control her gift. Holding in all that energy causes her intense pain.
She's nearly destroyed Afterlife a dozen times.
That's why we were so eager to help Elena. If there were a way to take away someone's gift, we had to know.
So you could take away Kora's.
It's better than the alternative. Not everyone here believes she's worth saving.
If she's capable of this, she isn't. And now she has his gun. You think she plans to use it for something good?
She's afraid of what you'll do to her, Li.
No. That's not what she's afraid of.
